It feels great. It really does. I was not born with high standards for following through on everything. I was not. I developed them in my teens, in my, in my teens. That's when it really happened. When I equated making lots of money with my own hard work, I did it when I was younger. I had jobs, you know, let me age stand. I'm talking about on the beach. I worked an hour and 10 minutes sold out a cooler, made 120 bucks, went back the next day sold out four calls, made 500 bucks. I realized the harder I worked, the more I make. I started doing that every single day. Next year I had bagels buttered by my mother and I woke up at 6 a.m. and I went early to the beach by 8 o'clock. I was on the beach selling bagels. Well, the sun really got hot, you know, high in the sky. The point was is I had this undeniable linkage, bold, bright, that I work hard, I get what I want in life.
